title: Copy-Paste

content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N, the length of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single integer – the minimal possible length of the original array – on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First array contains only one distinct value, so the minimal original array length is 1. The second array has distinct values {1,2,3}; starting from [1,2,3] we can copy the prefix [1,2] and paste it at the end to obtain the final array.
All three values are different, therefore the initial array must contain all three.
Four distinct numbers require an initial array of length four.

solution.approach: The only operation allowed is copying a contiguous subsequence and inserting it somewhere else. Any element that appears in the final array must have been present in the original array, therefore the original array must contain at least all distinct values of A. Conversely, starting from an array that consists exactly of the distinct values (in any order that respects their first occurrence) we can obtain A by repeatedly copying appropriate subsequences, because each copy can reproduce any occurrence of an already existing element. Hence the minimal possible length equals the number of distinct integers in A. The solution therefore counts distinct values for each test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (reading the array and inserting into a hash set).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(D) where D is the number of distinct values (at most N).
